item.title: AirAsia Tests Market With Deutsche-Led $230 Million Private Debt
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG is marketing a $230 million private-credit deal for Malaysian budget airline AirAsia Aviation Group, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand for the carrier amid rising fuel prices.

item.title: Taiwan Weighs US Dollar Dividend Payouts for Listed Companies
item.description: Taiwan is weighing a proposal to permit listed companies to distribute dividends in US dollars, a change from the rule requiring payouts in the local currency, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Markets Get in Tune to the Strait and Narrow
item.description: Earnings hopes counter the volatile lack of message discipline by the US and Iran.

item.title: Siemens Warns EU’s AI Rules Will Deter Investment in Europe
item.description: Siemens AG will prioritize artificial-intelligence investments in the US and China if the European Union doesn’t adapt its restrictive regulations, according to Chief Executive Officer Roland Busch.

item.title: China’s Netflix iQiyi Goes All-In on AI Content in Big Overhaul
item.description: IQiyi Inc. expects AI to create a big chunk of its films and shows virtually from scratch someday soon, a monumental industry shift that spurred the Netflix-style streaming service to begin the biggest corporate overhaul in its 16-year history.

item.title: Singapore Is Procuring More LNG as Iran War Cuts Some Supply
item.description: Singapore is getting additional liquefied natural gas from outside of the Middle East, as the war in Iran chokes supply from the region, according to a government body.

item.title: Malaysia Cautious as War-Led Inflation to Hit Later This Year
item.description: Malaysia is bracing for a delayed uptick in inflation as the impact of the Iran war filters through to consumer prices later this year, according to Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

item.title: New Zealand Exports Hit Monthly Record on Dairy, China Demand
item.description: New Zealand’s exports hit an all-time high in March as the trade-dependent economy offset the early impact of the Iran war with bumper dairy sales overseas.

item.title: Takaichi’s Ratings Stay High With Some Wavering Amid Iran Impact
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s popularity ratings remained high by historical standards despite a slight dip in some polls as the public weighed her handling of the impact of the war in Iran.

item.title: Major Japanese Life Insurer to Slow Buying of Domestic Debt
item.description: Fukoku Mutual Life Insurance Co. plans to slow purchases of Japanese government debt this fiscal year as it sees limited upside in super-long bond yields.

item.title: Indian Stocks’ Rally Faces Test as Oil Jumps on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
item.description: Nifty capped a second straight weekly advance on Friday.

item.title: China’s Government Urges ‘Every Effort’ to Curb Solar Capacity
item.description: Chinese authorities have called for “every effort” to strengthen capacity controls in the solar industry, as the sector continues to struggle with excess production.

item.title: Singapore Urges Banks to Fix Security Gaps Amid Mythos AI Fears
item.description: Singapore’s financial regulator is urging banks to plug their cybersecurity holes, as concerns over Anthropic PBC’s latest AI model Mythos spread to Asia.

item.title: Japan’s Opposition Urges 5-Year Path to Achieve Primary Surplus
item.description: The leader of one of Japan’s smaller opposition parties said that while a deficit in the primary balance is acceptable in the near term, the government should present a five-year road map toward achieving a surplus by around 2030 as interest rates are poised to rise.

item.title: Korean Stocks Erase War Loss as Chipmakers Rise on AI Resurgence
item.description: South Korean stocks have erased the slide triggered by the Iran war, driven by a rally in chipmakers as the artificial intelligence trade swings back into investor focus.

item.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Erase War Losses on AI Optimism
item.description: Emerging-market equities fully recovered their losses from the Iran war, as optimism over artificial intelligence trades lifted Asian stocks.

item.title: Copper Drops From Two-Month High as US-Iran Tensions Escalate
item.description: Copper fell from its highest close since early February, as ceasefire talks between Washington and Tehran were thrown into uncertainty after the US seized an Iranian ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: World’s Most Expensive Broking Stock Faces Earnings Test After Doubling
item.description: Investors will watch Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd.’s earnings Monday for evidence its results justify a 100% jump in the stock since its November initial public offering — a surge that has made it the world’s most expensive brokerage.

item.title: AI’s Token Economy Revolution Creates New China Tech Winners
item.description: China’s cheap artificial intelligence models are rapidly attracting global users and creating new winners in the nation’s stock market.

item.title: Hong Kong Exchange Tightens Rules to Avoid Auditor Shopping
item.description: Hong Kong listed companies must now obtain shareholder approval to change auditors, part of a regulatory push to bolster corporate governance and transparency in the $7.5 trillion market.

item.title: China Revives Coal-to-Gas Projects as Energy Security Frays
item.description: A high-profile Chinese coal-to-gas venture that lay dormant for over a decade is set to launch this year, part of a wave of investments that will allow Beijing to mitigate threats to fuel supply at a time of heightened geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Bank of Korea’s Outgoing Governor Flags Limits to Policy Tools
item.description: South Korea’s outgoing central bank governor said monetary and fiscal policy are becoming less sufficient as primary tools for steering the economy, urging structural reforms as he steps down after a four-year term in which he dealt with repeated systemic shocks.

item.title: China to Begin Special Bond Sales With Record 30-Year Offering
item.description: China will kick off this year’s ultra-long special government bond sales with a record offering of 30-year notes on Friday, in a move that will offer a gauge of investor demand.

item.title: Australian Pension Chiefs Target Private Assets in European Trip
item.description: Australia’s biggest pension funds are heading to France and the UK this week in a fresh push for private market deals, just over a month after a similar US trip.

item.title: Malaysian Ringgit Set to Test New High for 2026, Strategists Say
item.description: The Malaysian ringgit is likely to retest its year-to-date peak versus the dollar thanks to strong fundamentals, according to strategists.

item.title: Asia Hedge Funds Log Big Losses From Iran War Before Truce Rally
item.description: To see how Asian hedge funds got blindsided by the Iran war, look no further than Trivest Advisors Ltd.

item.title: Australia’s Viva Sees Refinery Output at 90% Within Weeks
item.description: Viva Energy Group Ltd. shares fell after the company said its Geelong refinery in Australia will boost diesel, jet fuel and gasoline production to about 90% of capacity over the next few weeks after a major fire forced it to cut output.

item.title: Bill Ackman, The Weeknd and the Hunt for Universal’s Missing Billions
item.description: Bill Ackman has offered to buy Universal Music Group. What he really wants is for the stock to go up.

item.title: Singapore’s Model for Success Faces Test in a Less Global Era
item.description: Singapore’s growth is poised to moderate as its export-driven model is strained by geopolitical tensions and a fragmenting global trading system, though it could draw support from opportunities in the Middle East, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Asia Private Credit Debates Changes to Soothe Jittery Investors
item.description: Some of Asia’s private credit firms are mulling changes to the industry including longer lock-up periods and higher redemption caps after turmoil in the US in recent months increased scrutiny from investors and regulators across the region.
